patch:
diff --git a/setup_dkms_linked.py b/setup_dkms_linked.py
new file mode 100644
index 000000000..855dcf6d3
--- /dev/null
+++ b/setup_dkms_linked.py
@@ -0,0 +1,86 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env python3
+"""
+Creates a DKMS source tree with symlinks to the mainline kernel amdgpu sources.
+Mirrors drivers/gpu/drm/amd/ layout so kbuild's $(src)/.. resolves correctly.
+
+Usage:
+    python3 setup_dkms_linked.py
+    sudo ln -sf $(pwd)/amdgpu-dkms /usr/src/amdgpu-1.0
+    sudo dkms install amdgpu/1.0
+"""
+
+import os
+import shutil
+
+DEST_DIR = "amdgpu-dkms"
+AMD_SRC = "drivers/gpu/drm/amd"
+AMD_SUBDIRS = ["amdgpu", "amdkfd", "amdxcp", "display", "include", "pm", "acp", "ras"]
+
+DKMS_CONF = """\
+PACKAGE_NAME="amdgpu"
+PACKAGE_VERSION="1.0"
+MAKE="make KERNELVER=${kernelver}"
+CLEAN="make clean KERNELVER=${kernelver}"
+BUILT_MODULE_NAME[0]="amdgpu"
+BUILT_MODULE_LOCATION[0]="amd/amdgpu"
+DEST_MODULE_LOCATION[0]="/kernel/drivers/gpu/drm/amd/amdgpu"
+BUILT_MODULE_NAME[1]="amdxcp"
+BUILT_MODULE_LOCATION[1]="amd/amdxcp"
+DEST_MODULE_LOCATION[1]="/kernel/drivers/gpu/drm/amd/amdxcp"
+AUTOINSTALL="yes"
+"""
+
+MAKEFILE = """\
+KERNELVER ?= $(shell uname -r)
+KDIR := /lib/modules/$(KERNELVER)/build
+
+all:
+\t$(MAKE) -C $(KDIR) M=$(CURDIR)/amd/amdgpu CONFIG_DRM_AMDGPU=m modules
+\t$(MAKE) -C $(KDIR) M=$(CURDIR)/amd/amdxcp CONFIG_DRM_AMDGPU=m modules
+
+clean:
+\t$(MAKE) -C $(KDIR) M=$(CURDIR)/amd/amdgpu clean
+\t$(MAKE) -C $(KDIR) M=$(CURDIR)/amd/amdxcp clean
+"""
+
+
+def link_tree(src_dir, dest_dir):
+    """Recursively create directory structure and symlink individual files."""
+    os.makedirs(dest_dir, exist_ok=True)
+    for entry in os.listdir(src_dir):
+        src = os.path.join(src_dir, entry)
+        dst = os.path.join(dest_dir, entry)
+        if os.path.isdir(src):
+            link_tree(src, dst)
+        else:
+            if os.path.lexists(dst):
+                os.remove(dst)
+            os.symlink(os.path.abspath(src), dst)
+
+
+def main():
+    if os.path.exists(DEST_DIR):
+        shutil.rmtree(DEST_DIR)
+
+    amd_dest = os.path.join(DEST_DIR, "amd")
+
+    for subdir in AMD_SUBDIRS:
+        src = os.path.join(AMD_SRC, subdir)
+        dst = os.path.join(amd_dest, subdir)
+        if os.path.isdir(src):
+            link_tree(src, dst)
+            print(f"  linked {dst}/ -> {src}/")
+
+    with open(os.path.join(DEST_DIR, "dkms.conf"), "w") as f:
+        f.write(DKMS_CONF)
+    with open(os.path.join(DEST_DIR, "Makefile"), "w") as f:
+        f.write(MAKEFILE)
+
+    print(f"\nDKMS source tree created at {os.path.abspath(DEST_DIR)}")
+    print(f"\nTo install:")
+    print(f"  sudo ln -sf {os.path.abspath(DEST_DIR)} /usr/src/amdgpu-1.0")
+    print(f"  sudo dkms install amdgpu/1.0")
+
+
+if __name__ == "__main__":
+    main()
_reconstruction_warnings: [curator 2026-05-14] Verified the on-topic patch is complete: setup_dkms_linked.py (86 lines, +86/-0) creates a recursive symlink tree mirroring drivers/gpu/drm/amd/ plus dkms.conf + Makefile.  Ran the script against the sparse-checkout linux repo at base commit bc47b2e823914966c15a09422f8fc3aa98d34c1b: 2698 symlinks (0 broken, 0 outside), 624 targets in amdgpu/, 6/6 essential source probes found, dkms.conf + Makefile both present and shell-sourceable.  All 7 F2P gates in tests/test.sh pass with this canonical patch alone.  The 37 stripped tool_use ops referenced in the prior warnings are off-topic mid-session work (kfd_pc_sampling.c/h, netfilter modules, memory-model litmus) that the session veered into; harbor_tasks/linux-amdgpu-dkms-convert/instruction.md is scoped only to the DKMS symlink-tree generator, so dropping those edits is correct, not lossy.
